heroPoems.title: The Nameless
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The way you can explain
is not the constant Way.

heroPoems.title: Vision of the Cosmic Form
heroPoems.firstStanza:
You asked to see Me as I truly am.
No ordinary eyes can hold this vision.
I grant divine sight—look.

heroPoems.title: Sweeney Among the Nightingales
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: The Prairie
heroPoems.firstStanza:
'I see the grass shake in the sun for leagues on either hand,
I see a river loop and run about a treeless land--
An empty plain, a steely pond, a distance diamond-clear,
And low blue naked hills beyond. And what is that to fear?'

heroPoems.title: Brahma
heroPoems.firstStanza:
If the red slayer think he slays,
Or if the slain think he is slain,
They know not well the subtle ways
I keep, and pass, and turn again.
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: To Sleep
heroPoems.firstStanza:
O soft embalmer of the still midnight,
Shutting, with careful fingers and benign,
Our gloom-pleas'd eyes, embower'd from the light,
Enshaded in forgetfulness divine:
O soothest Sleep! if so it please thee, close,
In midst of this thine hymn my willing eyes,

heroPoems.title: Depth Calls
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Deep calls to deep
in the roar of your waters.
All your waves
break over me.

heroPoems.title: Sonnets to Orpheus II, 29
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Silent friend of many distances,
feel how your breathing enlarges space.
Let your presence ring
on the dark bell-wood.
Whatever feeds itself on you
will become strong.
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: How could the love between Thee and me sever?
heroPoems.firstStanza:
How could the love between Thee and me sever?
As the leaf of the lotus abides on the water: so thou art my
Lord, and I am Thy servant.
As the night-bird Chakor gazes all night at the moon: so Thou art
my Lord and I am Thy servant.
From the beginning until the ending of time, there is love

heroPoems.title: Every task feels impossible, and calling it easy is hardest of all.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Every task feels impossible, and calling it easy is hardest of all.
To become human itself is no small work.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

collections.name: A Field Behind Memory
collections.tagline: For evenings touched by old songs.
collections.theme: Language for the suspended hour between conversation and sleep.

collections.name: The Kindness of Empty Chairs
collections.tagline: For when pages smell like rain.
collections.theme: A gathering of poems where memory and present tense overlap gently.

featuredPoem.title: On Houses
featuredPoem.firstLines:
And he answered and said:
Build of your imaginings a bower in the wilderness ere you build a house within the city walls.

poems.title: In midsummer I walk with my staff
The Practice of Remembering in Battle

collections.name: What Lingers in Hallways
collections.theme: Poems that move through corridors, stairwells, and rooms as emotional architecture.

poems.title: I Am the Taste of Water
